input.prompt:
My MSTest project has a .testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout,
and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

data.content: `.testsettings` files are **not supported** by modern MSTest (v2+/v3). You need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file. Here's your configuration translated:

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <!-- Global test timeout (30 seconds) -->
  <RunConfiguration>
    <TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>
  </RunConfiguration>

  <!-- Per-test timeout for MSTest -->
  <MSTest>
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>

  <!-- Code coverage data collector -->
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.dll">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage />
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.reasoningText:
The user has a `.testsettings` file with:
1. Test deployment/assembly resolution settings
2. A test timeout of 30000ms (30 seconds)
3. A code coverage data collector

They've upgraded to MSTest 3.8.0. The `.testsettings` format is deprecated and not supported in modern MSTest. They need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file.

Let me create the equivalent `.runsettings` file for them and explain the migration.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <!-- Global test timeout (30 seconds) -->
  <RunConfiguration>
    <TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>
  </RunConfiguration>

  <!-- Per-test timeout for MSTest -->
  <MSTest>
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>

  <!-- Code coverage data collector -->
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.dll">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage />
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.content:
I've created `local.runsettings` with equivalent configuration. Here's what changed:

| `.testsettings` setting | `.runsettings` equivalent |
|---|---|
| `<Property name="TestTimeout" value="30000" />` | `<MSTest><TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout></MSTest>` |
| `AssemblyResolution / TestDirectory` | `<DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>` (deployment is off by default now) |
| `AgentRule > DataCollectors` (coverage v1.0) | `DataCollectionRunSettings > DataCollectors` (coverage v2.0) |

**To use the new file**, either:

1. **CLI:** `dotnet test --settings local.runsettings`
2. **In the `.csproj`:**
   ```xml
   <PropertyGroup>
     <RunSettingsFilePath>$(MSBuildProjectDirectory)\local.runsettings</RunSettingsFilePath>
   </PropertyGroup>
   ```

You can safely **delete `local.testsettings`** — it is completely ignored by MSTest v3.
